description: Python hashlib.md5 is cryptographically broken and must not be used for security purposes.
fix_hint: Use hashlib.sha256() or hashlib.sha3_256() instead of md5.

description: Python hashlib.sha1 is deprecated for security use due to practical collision attacks.
fix_hint: Use hashlib.sha256() or hashlib.sha3_256() instead of sha1.

description: Node.js crypto.createHash('md5') uses a broken hash algorithm vulnerable to collision attacks.
fix_hint: Use crypto.createHash('sha256') instead of md5.

description: Node.js crypto.createHash('sha1') uses a deprecated hash with known collision attacks.
fix_hint: Use crypto.createHash('sha256') instead of sha1.

description: Java MessageDigest MD5 is cryptographically broken and enables collision attacks.
fix_hint: Use MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256") instead.

description: Java MessageDigest SHA-1 is deprecated with demonstrated collision attacks.
fix_hint: Use MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256") instead.

description: Go crypto/md5 is broken and must not be used for integrity or authentication.
fix_hint: Use crypto/sha256 instead of crypto/md5.

description: Go crypto/sha1 is deprecated with practical collision attacks since 2017.
fix_hint: Use crypto/sha256 instead of crypto/sha1.

description: Ruby Digest::MD5 uses a broken hash algorithm vulnerable to collision attacks.
fix_hint: Use Digest::SHA256 instead of Digest::MD5.

description: Ruby Digest::SHA1 is deprecated due to practical collision attacks.
fix_hint: Use Digest::SHA256 instead of Digest::SHA1.

description: PHP md5() is cryptographically broken and must not be used for security.
fix_hint: Use hash('sha256', $data) or password_hash() for passwords.

description: PHP sha1() is deprecated for security use due to collision attacks.
fix_hint: Use hash('sha256', $data) instead of sha1().

description: C# MD5.Create() uses a broken hash algorithm that enables collision attacks.
fix_hint: Use SHA256.Create() instead of MD5.Create().

description: C# SHA1.Create() is deprecated due to known collision vulnerabilities.
fix_hint: Use SHA256.Create() instead of SHA1.Create().

description: C# MD5CryptoServiceProvider uses a broken hash and is obsolete.
fix_hint: Use SHA256Managed or SHA256.Create() instead.

description: Rust md5 crate uses a broken hash algorithm unfit for security purposes.
fix_hint: Use the sha2 crate (sha2::Sha256) instead of md5.

description: C# SHA1CryptoServiceProvider is deprecated due to SHA-1 collision attacks.
fix_hint: Use SHA256CryptoServiceProvider or SHA256.Create() instead.

description: Java DES cipher has only 56-bit keys and is trivially brute-forced.
fix_hint: Use Cipher.getInstance("AES/GCM/NoPadding") instead of DES.

description: Java 3DES (DESede) is deprecated by NIST and offers insufficient security margins.
fix_hint: Use Cipher.getInstance("AES/GCM/NoPadding") instead of DESede.

description: Java RC4/ARCFOUR cipher has known biases that leak plaintext bytes.
fix_hint: Use AES-GCM or ChaCha20-Poly1305 instead of RC4.

description: Python DES cipher has 56-bit keys that are trivially brute-forced.
fix_hint: Use AES from Crypto.Cipher instead of DES.

description: Python 3DES (DES3) is deprecated with insufficient security margins for modern use.
fix_hint: Use AES from Crypto.Cipher instead of DES3.

description: Python ARC4 (RC4) has known statistical biases that leak plaintext.
fix_hint: Use AES-GCM from Crypto.Cipher instead of ARC4.

description: .NET DESCryptoServiceProvider uses 56-bit keys that are trivially broken.
fix_hint: Use Aes.Create() instead of DESCryptoServiceProvider.

description: .NET RC2CryptoServiceProvider uses a weak cipher with known vulnerabilities.
fix_hint: Use Aes.Create() instead of RC2CryptoServiceProvider.

description: Java Blowfish cipher has a 64-bit block size vulnerable to birthday attacks.
fix_hint: Use Cipher.getInstance("AES/GCM/NoPadding") instead of Blowfish.

description: Python Blowfish has a 64-bit block size that is vulnerable to Sweet32 birthday attacks.
fix_hint: Use AES from Crypto.Cipher instead of Blowfish.

description: Java AES in ECB mode does not provide semantic security and leaks data patterns.
fix_hint: Use AES/GCM/NoPadding or AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding with a random IV.

description: Python AES in ECB mode reveals identical plaintext blocks in ciphertext.
fix_hint: Use AES.MODE_GCM or AES.MODE_CBC with a random IV instead.

description: .NET ECB cipher mode leaks patterns in encrypted data.
fix_hint: Use CipherMode.CBC or CipherMode.CFB with a random IV.

description: Go ECB mode encryption does not provide semantic security.
fix_hint: Use cipher.NewGCM() or cipher.NewCBCEncrypter() with a random IV.

description: Ruby OpenSSL AES-ECB mode leaks plaintext patterns in ciphertext.
fix_hint: Use aes-256-gcm or aes-256-cbc instead of ECB mode.

description: Hardcoded encryption key in source code enables trivial decryption by anyone with code access.
fix_hint: Load encryption keys from environment variables or a secrets manager.

description: Encryption key defined as a hex literal in source code allows anyone with repo access to decrypt data.
fix_hint: Load encryption keys from a vault or environment variable, never hardcode.

description: Hardcoded initialization vector (IV) removes randomness and may enable chosen-plaintext attacks.
fix_hint: Generate a random IV for each encryption operation using a CSPRNG.

description: Hardcoded nonce defeats authenticated encryption and can lead to key recovery in GCM mode.
fix_hint: Generate a unique random nonce for each encryption using a CSPRNG.

description: Base64-encoded key material in source code is trivially extractable.
fix_hint: Store key material in a secrets manager or KMS, not in source code.

description: Java SecretKeySpec initialized with a string literal embeds the key in the binary.
fix_hint: Load key material from a KeyStore or environment variable.

description: Go AES cipher initialized with a string literal key is extractable from the binary.
fix_hint: Load AES keys from environment variables or a secrets manager.

description: .NET cipher Key assigned from a static byte array embeds secret material in source.
fix_hint: Use Azure Key Vault or DPAPI to manage encryption keys.

description: 1024-bit RSA keys are factorable with modern hardware and NIST deprecated them in 2013.
fix_hint: Use at least 2048-bit RSA keys, preferably 4096-bit.

description: 512-bit RSA keys can be factored in hours and provide no security whatsoever.
fix_hint: Use at least 2048-bit RSA keys.

description: 768-bit RSA was factored in 2009 and provides no meaningful security.
fix_hint: Use at least 2048-bit RSA keys.

description: 1024-bit DSA keys are deprecated by NIST and vulnerable to factoring attacks.
fix_hint: Use at least 2048-bit DSA or switch to ECDSA with P-256.

description: Python random module used for password/token generation is predictable with a known seed.
fix_hint: Use secrets.token_urlsafe() or secrets.choice() for security-sensitive randomness.

description: Python random.randint used for token/OTP generation is predictable after observing outputs.
fix_hint: Use secrets.randbelow() for generating security-sensitive random integers.

description: Java java.util.Random used for security token generation is predictable from observed outputs.
fix_hint: Use java.security.SecureRandom for generating security-sensitive values.

description: Go math/rand used for secret generation is deterministic and predictable.
fix_hint: Use crypto/rand.Read() for generating security-sensitive random values.

description: C rand()/srand() for cryptographic purposes is trivially predictable.
fix_hint: Use platform CSPRNG: getrandom(), /dev/urandom, or BCryptGenRandom.

description: Ruby rand used for session or token generation is predictable.
fix_hint: Use SecureRandom.hex or SecureRandom.uuid for security-sensitive values.

description: Math.random() used for security-sensitive value is predictable and not cryptographically secure.
fix_hint: Use crypto.randomBytes() or crypto.randomUUID() for security-sensitive values.

description: C# System.Random for security values is predictable from observed outputs.
fix_hint: Use System.Security.Cryptography.RandomNumberGenerator instead.

description: bcrypt with cost factor below 10 is too fast and vulnerable to GPU-accelerated brute force.
fix_hint: Use a bcrypt cost factor of at least 12 (recommended: 12-14).

description: PBKDF2 with fewer than 100000 iterations is too fast for password hashing.
fix_hint: Use at least 600000 iterations for PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 per OWASP 2023.

description: scrypt with N parameter below 2^14 is too fast for modern GPU attacks on passwords.
fix_hint: Use scrypt with N >= 2^15 (32768) for adequate password protection.

description: Argon2 with memory below 64MB weakens its resistance to GPU and ASIC attacks.
fix_hint: Use Argon2id with at least 64MB (65536 KB) memory cost.

description: Plain SHA-256/SHA-512 for password hashing is too fast and lacks salt iteration.
fix_hint: Use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2id for password hashing.

description: Single-round hash function for password storage allows fast brute-force attacks.
fix_hint: Use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2id which are designed for password hashing.

description: Python requests with verify=False disables certificate validation enabling MITM attacks.
fix_hint: Remove verify=False or set verify to a CA bundle path.

description: Python ssl._create_unverified_context disables all certificate checks.
fix_hint: Use ssl.create_default_context() which verifies certificates.

description: NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 disables TLS verification for the entire Node.js process.
fix_hint: Remove this setting and fix the certificate issue instead.

description: Node.js rejectUnauthorized: false disables TLS certificate validation on the connection.
fix_hint: Use a proper CA certificate or set rejectUnauthorized to true.

description: Go InsecureSkipVerify: true disables TLS certificate chain and hostname validation.
fix_hint: Remove InsecureSkipVerify or set it to false with proper CA certs.

description: Java trust-all certificate manager accepts any certificate enabling MITM attacks.
fix_hint: Use the default TrustManager with a proper truststore.

description: Ruby VERIFY_NONE disables all TLS certificate verification.
fix_hint: Use OpenSSL::SSL::VERIFY_PEER to validate certificates.

description: PHP cURL with VERIFYPEER false accepts any certificate enabling MITM.
fix_hint: Set CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYPEER to true and provide a CA bundle.

description: C# ServerCertificateValidationCallback always returning true disables TLS verification.
fix_hint: Implement proper certificate validation or remove the callback override.

description: SSLv3 is broken by the POODLE attack and must not be used.
fix_hint: Use TLS 1.2 or 1.3 only.

description: TLS 1.0 is deprecated by RFC 8996 and vulnerable to BEAST and other attacks.
fix_hint: Set minimum TLS version to 1.2.

description: TLS 1.1 is deprecated by RFC 8996 and lacks modern cipher suite support.
fix_hint: Set minimum TLS version to 1.2.

description: RC4 cipher suite in TLS has known biases that leak plaintext.
fix_hint: Use TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 or TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384 cipher suites.

description: NULL cipher suite provides no encryption and transmits data in plaintext.
fix_hint: Remove NULL cipher suites and use only AEAD ciphers.

description: EXPORT cipher suites use intentionally weakened 40/56-bit keys trivially broken by FREAK/Logjam.
fix_hint: Remove EXPORT cipher suites entirely.

description: Custom XOR cipher implementation provides no real security against cryptanalysis.
fix_hint: Use a vetted library with AES-GCM or ChaCha20-Poly1305.

description: XOR loop with key material is a homebrew cipher vulnerable to known-plaintext attacks.
fix_hint: Use AES-GCM from a standard crypto library instead of XOR encryption.

description: ROT13/Caesar cipher is a substitution cipher with zero cryptographic security.
fix_hint: Use AES-GCM or another authenticated encryption scheme.

description: Custom encryption function suggests homebrew cryptography which is almost always insecure.
fix_hint: Use a well-tested crypto library (NaCl/libsodium, OpenSSL, Web Crypto API).

description: Password hashed without salt allows rainbow table and precomputation attacks.
fix_hint: Use bcrypt/scrypt/Argon2id which include automatic salting.

description: Static/hardcoded salt negates the purpose of salting and enables precomputation attacks.
fix_hint: Generate a unique random salt per password using a CSPRNG.

description: Empty salt is equivalent to no salt and allows rainbow table attacks.
fix_hint: Generate a unique random salt of at least 16 bytes per password.

description: Direct comparison of hashes/MACs leaks information through timing side channels.
fix_hint: Use crypto.timingSafeEqual (Node), hmac.compare_digest (Python), or MessageDigest.isEqual (Java).

description: Direct equality comparison of authentication tokens enables timing attacks to guess valid tokens.
fix_hint: Use a constant-time comparison function for token validation.

description: Direct comparison of password hashes enables timing side-channel attacks.
fix_hint: Use bcrypt.compare, crypto.timingSafeEqual, or hmac.compare_digest.

description: Go bytes.Equal for MAC/token comparison is not constant-time.
fix_hint: Use crypto/subtle.ConstantTimeCompare() for MAC and token comparisons.

description: Ruby eql? for secret comparison is not constant-time and leaks length/content info.
fix_hint: Use ActiveSupport::SecurityUtils.secure_compare or Rack::Utils.secure_compare.

description: Disabling hostname verification allows MITM even with valid but wrong certificates.
fix_hint: Enable hostname verification to prevent MITM with valid certificates.

description: Trusting self-signed certificates in production bypasses the CA trust model.
fix_hint: Use certificates from a trusted CA or pin the specific certificate.

description: Disabled certificate pinning allows attacks using rogue CA-signed certificates.
fix_hint: Enable certificate pinning for sensitive connections.

description: Anonymous Diffie-Hellman cipher suites provide no server authentication.
fix_hint: Remove anonymous cipher suites and use only authenticated AEAD ciphers.

description: Java X509TrustManager with empty checkServerTrusted accepts any certificate.
fix_hint: Use the default TrustManagerFactory or implement proper certificate validation.
